text:
Market today:
The market's got a mixed vibe today, with a lot of political noise from Trump headlines. For our tech/chip heavy book, the real driver to watch is the potential impact of SpaceX's Nasdaq-100 inclusion on index rebalancing and options pricing.

Your holdings:
Two positions need immediate attention: SOXL is down 22% and trading below its default stop at $200. SPAL, a new leveraged ETF, is down 16.5% and also below its $21.38 stop. Remember, with margin, these drawdowns hit extra hard.

Watch:
Keep an eye on CRWD for a potential add if it confirms its scanner setup around $198.22, and monitor ASML for accumulation around $1832.62 given its chip sector relevance.

Bottom line:
Focus on managing your leveraged positions at their stops and validate any new tech/chip entries with conviction.
plays: Trim SOXL: Trading below its $200 stop at $198.52 and down 22%. Respect the stop on this leveraged position.
Trim SPAL: This new leveraged ETF is trading below its $21.38 stop at $21 and down 16.5%. Manage risk on this volatile holding.
Consider ASML: Scanner shows an 'ACCUMULATE' setup (entry 1832.62-1830.78, stop 1826.63). A chip name, fits your book.
